verses.sanskrit: सूत उवाचइति भीत: प्रजाद्रोहात्सर्वधर्मविवित्सया ।ततो विनशनं प्रागाद् यत्र देवव्रतोऽपतत् ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: sūta uvācaiti bhītaḥ prajā-drohātsarva-dharma-vivitsayātato vinaśanaṁ prāgādyatra deva-vrato ’patat
verses.text: Sūta Gosvāmī said: Being afraid for having killed so many subjects on the Battlefield of Kurukṣetra, Mahārāja Yudhiṣṭhira went to the scene of the massacre. There, Bhīṣmadeva was lying on a bed of arrows, about to pass away.

verses.sanskrit: तदा ते भ्रातर: सर्वे सदश्वै: स्वर्णभूषितै: ।अन्वगच्छन् रथैर्विप्रा व्यासधौम्यादयस्तथा ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: tadā te bhrātaraḥ sarvesadaśvaiḥ svarṇa-bhūṣitaiḥanvagacchan rathair viprāvyāsa-dhaumyādayas tathā
verses.text: At that time all his brothers followed him on beautiful chariots drawn by first-class horses decorated with gold ornaments. With them were Vyāsa and ṛṣis like Dhaumya [the learned priest of the Pāṇḍavas] and others.

verses.sanskrit: भगवानपि विप्रर्षे रथेन सधनञ्जय: ।स तैर्व्यरोचत नृप: कुवेर इव गुह्यकै: ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: bhagavān api viprarṣerathena sa-dhanañjayaḥsa tair vyarocata nṛpaḥkuvera iva guhyakaiḥ
verses.text: O sage amongst the brāhmaṇas, Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa, the Personality of Godhead, also followed, seated on a chariot with Arjuna. Thus King Yudhiṣṭhira appeared very aristocratic, like Kuvera surrounded by his companions [the Guhyakas].

verses.sanskrit: द‍ृष्ट्वा निपतितं भूमौ दिवश्‍च्युतमिवामरम् ।प्रणेमु: पाण्डवा भीष्मं सानुगा: सह चक्रिणा ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: dṛṣṭvā nipatitaṁ bhūmaudivaś cyutam ivāmarampraṇemuḥ pāṇḍavā bhīṣmaṁsānugāḥ saha cakriṇā
verses.text: Seeing him [Bhīṣma] lying on the ground, like a demigod fallen from the sky, the Pāṇḍava King Yudhiṣṭhira, along with his younger brothers and Lord Kṛṣṇa, bowed down before him.

verses.sanskrit: तत्र ब्रह्मर्षय: सर्वे देवर्षयश्च सत्तम ।राजर्षयश्च तत्रासन् द्रष्टुं भरतपुङ्गवम् ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: tatra brahmarṣayaḥ sarvedevarṣayaś ca sattamarājarṣayaś ca tatrāsandraṣṭuṁ bharata-puṅgavam
verses.text: Just to see the chief of the descendants of King Bharata [Bhīṣma], all the great souls in the universe, namely the ṛṣis amongst the demigods, brāhmaṇas and kings, all situated in the quality of goodness, were assembled there.

verses.sanskrit: पर्वतो नारदो धौम्यो भगवान् बादरायण: ।बृहदश्वो भरद्वाज: सशिष्यो रेणुकासुत: ॥ ६ ॥वसिष्ठ इन्द्रप्रमदस्त्रितो गृत्समदोऽसित: ।कक्षीवान् गौतमोऽत्रिश्च कौशिकोऽथ सुदर्शन: ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: parvato nārado dhaumyobhagavān bādarāyaṇaḥbṛhadaśvo bharadvājaḥsaśiṣyo reṇukā-sutaḥ
verses.text: All the sages like Parvata Muni, Nārada, Dhaumya, Vyāsa the incarnation of God, Bṛhadaśva, Bharadvāja and Paraśurāma and disciples, Vasiṣṭha, Indrapramada, Trita, Gṛtsamada, Asita, Kakṣīvān, Gautama, Atri, Kauśika and Sudarśana were present.

verses.sanskrit: अन्ये च मुनयो ब्रह्मन् ब्रह्मरातादयोऽमला: ।शिष्यैरुपेता आजग्मु: कश्यपाङ्गिरसादय: ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: anye ca munayo brahmanbrahmarātādayo ’malāḥśiṣyair upetā ājagmuḥkaśyapāṅgirasādayaḥ
verses.text: And many others like Śukadeva Gosvāmī and other purified souls, Kaśyapa and Āṅgirasa and others, all accompanied by their respective disciples, arrived there.

verses.sanskrit: तान् समेतान् महाभागानुपलभ्य वसूत्तम: ।पूजयामास धर्मज्ञो देशकालविभागवित् ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: tān sametān mahā-bhāgānupalabhya vasūttamaḥpūjayām āsa dharma-jñodeśa-kāla-vibhāgavit
verses.text: Bhīṣmadeva, who was the best amongst the eight Vasus, received and welcomed all the great and powerful ṛṣis who were assembled there, for he knew perfectly all the religious principles according to time and place.

verses.sanskrit: कृष्णं च तत्प्रभावज्ञ आसीनं जगदीश्वरम् ।हृदिस्थं पूजयामास माययोपात्तविग्रहम् ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: kṛṣṇaṁ ca tat-prabhāva-jñaāsīnaṁ jagad-īśvaramhṛdi-sthaṁ pūjayām āsamāyayopātta-vigraham
verses.text: Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa is situated in everyone’s heart, yet He manifests His transcendental form by His internal potency. This very Lord was sitting before Bhīṣmadeva, and since Bhīṣmadeva knew of His glories, he worshiped Him duly.

verses.sanskrit: पाण्डुपुत्रानुपासीनान् प्रश्रयप्रेमसङ्गतान् ।अभ्याचष्टानुरागाश्रैरन्धीभूतेन चक्षुषा ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: pāṇḍu-putrān upāsīnānpraśraya-prema-saṅgatānabhyācaṣṭānurāgāśrairandhībhūtena cakṣuṣā
verses.text: The sons of Mahārāja Pāṇḍu were sitting silently nearby, overtaken with affection for their dying grandfather. Seeing this, Bhīṣmadeva congratulated them with feeling. There were tears of ecstasy in his eyes, for he was overwhelmed by love and affection.

verses.sanskrit: अहो कष्टमहोऽन्याय्यं यद्यूयं धर्मनन्दना: ।जीवितुं नार्हथ क्लिष्टं विप्रधर्माच्युताश्रया: ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: aho kaṣṭam aho ’nyāyyaṁyad yūyaṁ dharma-nandanāḥjīvituṁ nārhatha kliṣṭaṁvipra-dharmācyutāśrayāḥ
verses.text: Bhīṣmadeva said: Oh, what terrible sufferings and what terrible injustices you good souls suffer for being the sons of religion personified. You did not deserve to remain alive under those tribulations, yet you were protected by the brāhmaṇas, God and religion.

verses.sanskrit: संस्थितेऽतिरथे पाण्डौ पृथा बालप्रजा वधू: ।युष्मत्कृते बहून् क्लेशान् प्राप्ता तोकवती मुहु: ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: saṁsthite ’tirathe pāṇḍaupṛthā bāla-prajā vadhūḥyuṣmat-kṛte bahūn kleśānprāptā tokavatī muhuḥ
verses.text: As far as my daughter-in-law Kuntī is concerned, upon the great General Pāṇḍu’s death, she became a widow with many children, and therefore she suffered greatly. And when you were grown up she suffered a great deal also because of your actions.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वं कालकृतं मन्ये भवतां च यदप्रियम् ।सपालो यद्वशे लोको वायोरिव घनावलि: ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: sarvaṁ kāla-kṛtaṁ manyebhavatāṁ ca yad-apriyamsapālo yad-vaśe lokovāyor iva ghanāvaliḥ
verses.text: In my opinion, this is all due to inevitable time, under whose control everyone in every planet is carried, just as the clouds are carried by the wind.

verses.sanskrit: यत्र धर्मसुतो राजा गदापाणिर्वृकोदर: ।कृष्णोऽस्त्री गाण्डिवं चापं सुहृत्कृष्णस्ततो विपत् ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: yatra dharma-suto rājāgadā-pāṇir vṛkodaraḥkṛṣṇo ’strī gāṇḍivaṁ cāpaṁsuhṛt kṛṣṇas tato vipat
verses.text: Oh, how wonderful is the influence of inevitable time! It is irreversible — otherwise, how can there be reverses in the presence of King Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of the demigod controlling religion; Bhīma, the great fighter with a club; the great bowman Arjuna with his mighty weapon Gāṇḍīva; and above all, the Lord, the direct well-wisher of the Pāṇḍavas?

verses.sanskrit: न ह्यस्य कर्हिचिद्राजन् पुमान् वेद विधित्सितम् ।यद्विजिज्ञासया युक्ता मुह्यन्ति कवयोऽपि हि ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: na hy asya karhicid rājanpumān veda vidhitsitamyad vijijñāsayā yuktāmuhyanti kavayo ’pi hi
verses.text: O King, no one can know the plan of the Lord [Śrī Kṛṣṇa]. Even though great philosophers inquire exhaustively, they are bewildered.

verses.sanskrit: तस्मादिदं दैवतन्त्रं व्यवस्य भरतर्षभ ।तस्यानुविहितोऽनाथा नाथ पाहि प्रजा: प्रभो ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasmād idaṁ daiva-tantraṁvyavasya bharatarṣabhatasyānuvihito ’nāthānātha pāhi prajāḥ prabho
verses.text: O best among the descendants of Bharata [Yudhiṣṭhira], I maintain, therefore, that all this is within the plan of the Lord. Accepting the inconceivable plan of the Lord, you must follow it. You are now the appointed administrative head, and, my lord, you should now take care of those subjects who are now rendered helpless.

verses.sanskrit: एष वै भगवान्साक्षादाद्यो नारायण: पुमान् ।मोहयन्मायया लोकं गूढश्चरति वृष्णिषु ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: eṣa vai bhagavān sākṣādādyo nārāyaṇaḥ pumānmohayan māyayā lokaṁgūḍhaś carati vṛṣṇiṣu
verses.text: This Śrī Kṛṣṇa is no other than the inconceivable, original Personality of Godhead. He is the first Nārāyaṇa, the supreme enjoyer. But He is moving amongst the descendants of King Vṛṣṇi just like one of us, and He is bewildering us with His self-created energy.

verses.sanskrit: अस्यानुभावं भगवान् वेद गुह्यतमं शिव: ।देवर्षिर्नारद: साक्षाद्भगवान् कपिलो नृप ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: asyānubhāvaṁ bhagavānveda guhyatamaṁ śivaḥdevarṣir nāradaḥ sākṣādbhagavān kapilo nṛpa
verses.text: O King, Lord Śiva, Nārada the sage amongst the demigods, and Kapila, the incarnation of Godhead, all know very confidentially about His glories through direct contact.

verses.sanskrit: यं मन्यसे मातुलेयं प्रियं मित्रं सुहृत्तमम् ।अकरो: सचिवं दूतं सौहृदादथ सारथिम् ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: yaṁ manyase mātuleyaṁpriyaṁ mitraṁ suhṛttamamakaroḥ sacivaṁ dūtaṁsauhṛdād atha sārathim
verses.text: O King, that personality whom, out of ignorance only, you thought to be your maternal cousin, your very dear friend, well-wisher, counselor, messenger, benefactor, etc., is that very Personality of Godhead Śrī Kṛṣṇa.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वात्मन: समद‍ृशो ह्यद्वयस्यानहङ्‍कृते: ।तत्कृतं मतिवैषम्यं निरवद्यस्य न क्‍वचित् ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: sarvātmanaḥ sama-dṛśohy advayasyānahaṅkṛteḥtat-kṛtaṁ mati-vaiṣamyaṁniravadyasya na kvacit
verses.text: Being the Absolute Personality of Godhead, He is present in everyone’s heart. He is equally kind to everyone, and He is free from the false ego of differentiation. Therefore whatever He does is free from material inebriety. He is equibalanced.

verses.sanskrit: तथाप्येकान्तभक्तेषु पश्य भूपानुकम्पितम् ।यन्मेऽसूंस्त्यजत: साक्षात्कृष्णो दर्शनमागत: ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: tathāpy ekānta-bhakteṣupaśya bhūpānukampitamyan me ’sūṁs tyajataḥ sākṣātkṛṣṇo darśanam āgataḥ
verses.text: Yet, despite His being equally kind to everyone, He has graciously come before me while I am ending my life, for I am His unflinching servitor.

verses.sanskrit: भक्त्यावेश्य मनो यस्मिन् वाचा यन्नाम कीर्तयन् ।त्यजन् कलेवरं योगी मुच्यते कामकर्मभि: ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: bhaktyāveśya mano yasminvācā yan-nāma kīrtayantyajan kalevaraṁ yogīmucyate kāma-karmabhiḥ
verses.text: The Personality of Godhead, who appears in the mind of the devotee by attentive devotion and meditation and by chanting of the holy name, releases the devotee from the bondage of fruitive activities at the time of his quitting the material body.

verses.sanskrit: स देवदेवो भगवान् प्रतीक्षतांकलेवरं यावदिदं हिनोम्यहम् ।प्रसन्नहासारुणलोचनोल्लस-न्मुखाम्बुजो ध्यानपथश्चतुर्भुज: ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa deva-devo bhagavān pratīkṣatāṁkalevaraṁ yāvad idaṁ hinomy ahamprasanna-hāsāruṇa-locanollasan-mukhāmbujo dhyāna-pathaś catur-bhujaḥ
verses.text: May my Lord, who is four-handed and whose beautifully decorated lotus face, with eyes as red as the rising sun, is smiling, kindly await me at that moment when I quit this material body.

verses.sanskrit: सूत उवाचयुधिष्ठिरस्तदाकर्ण्य शयानं शरपञ्जरे ।अपृच्छद्विविधान्धर्मानृषीणां चानुश‍ृण्वताम् ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: sūta uvācayudhiṣṭhiras tad ākarṇyaśayānaṁ śara-pañjareapṛcchad vividhān dharmānṛṣīṇāṁ cānuśṛṇvatām
verses.text: Sūta Gosvāmī said: Mahārāja Yudhiṣṭhira, after hearing Bhīṣmadeva speak in that appealing tone, asked him, in the presence of all the great ṛṣis, about the essential principles of various religious duties.

verses.sanskrit: पुरुषस्वभावविहितान् यथावर्णं यथाश्रमम् ।वैराग्यरागोपाधिभ्यामाम्नातोभयलक्षणान् ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: puruṣa-sva-bhāva-vihitānyathā-varṇaṁ yathāśramamvairāgya-rāgopādhibhyāmāmnātobhaya-lakṣaṇān
verses.text: At Mahārāja Yudhiṣṭhira’s inquiry, Bhīṣmadeva first defined all the classifications of castes and orders of life in terms of the individual’s qualifications. Then he systematically, in twofold divisions, described counteraction by detachment and interaction by attachment.

verses.sanskrit: दानधर्मान् राजधर्मान् मोक्षधर्मान् विभागश: ।स्त्रीधर्मान् भगवद्धर्मान् समासव्यासयोगत: ॥ २७ ॥
verses.transliteration: dāna-dharmān rāja-dharmānmokṣa-dharmān vibhāgaśaḥstrī-dharmān bhagavad-dharmānsamāsa-vyāsa-yogataḥ
verses.text: He then explained, by divisions, acts of charity, the pragmatic activities of a king and activities for salvation. Then he described the duties of women and devotees, both briefly and extensively.

verses.sanskrit: धर्मार्थकाममोक्षांश्च सहोपायान् यथा मुने ।नानाख्यानेतिहासेषु वर्णयामास तत्त्ववित् ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: dharmārtha-kāma-mokṣāṁś casahopāyān yathā munenānākhyānetihāseṣuvarṇayām āsa tattvavit
verses.text: Then he described the occupational duties of different orders and statuses of life, citing instances from history, for he was himself well acquainted with the truth.

verses.sanskrit: धर्मं प्रवदतस्तस्य स काल: प्रत्युपस्थित: ।यो योगिनश्छन्दमृत्योर्वाञ्छितस्तूत्तरायण: ॥ २९ ॥
verses.transliteration: dharmaṁ pravadatas tasyasa kālaḥ pratyupasthitaḥyo yoginaś chanda-mṛtyorvāñchitas tūttarāyaṇaḥ
verses.text: While Bhīṣmadeva was describing occupational duties, the sun’s course ran into the northern hemisphere. This period is desired by mystics who die at their will.

verses.sanskrit: तदोपसंहृत्य गिर: सहस्रणी-र्विमुक्तसङ्गं मन आदिपूरुषे ।कृष्णे लसत्पीतपटे चतुर्भुजेपुर:स्थितेऽमीलितद‍ृग्व्यधारयत् ॥ ३० ॥
verses.transliteration: tadopasaṁhṛtya giraḥ sahasraṇīrvimukta-saṅgaṁ mana ādi-pūruṣekṛṣṇe lasat-pīta-paṭe catur-bhujepuraḥ sthite ’mīlita-dṛg vyadhārayat
verses.text: Thereupon that man who spoke on different subjects with thousands of meanings, and who fought on thousands of battlefields and protected thousands of men, stopped speaking and, being completely freed from all bondage, withdrew his mind from everything else and fixed his wide-open eyes upon the original Personality of Godhead, Śrī Kṛṣṇa, who stood before him, four-handed, dressed in yellow garments that glittered and shined.

verses.sanskrit: विशुद्धया धारणया हताशुभ-स्तदीक्षयैवाशु गतायुधश्रम: ।निवृत्तसर्वेन्द्रियवृत्तिविभ्रम-स्तुष्टाव जन्यं विसृजञ्जनार्दनम् ॥ ३१ ॥
verses.transliteration: viśuddhayā dhāraṇayā hatāśubhastad-īkṣayaivāśu gatā-yudha-śramaḥnivṛtta-sarvendriya-vṛtti-vibhramastuṣṭāva janyaṁ visṛjañ janārdanam
verses.text: By pure meditation, looking at Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa, he at once was freed from all material inauspiciousness and was relieved of all bodily pains caused by the arrow wounds. Thus all the external activities of his senses at once stopped, and he prayed transcendentally to the controller of all living beings while quitting his material body.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीभीष्म उवाचइति मतिरुपकल्पिता वितृष्णाभगवति सात्वतपुङ्गवे विभूम्नि ।स्वसुखमुपगते क्‍वचिद्विहर्तुंप्रकृतिमुपेयुषि यद्भ‍वप्रवाह: ॥ ३२ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhīṣma uvācaiti matir upakalpitā vitṛṣṇābhagavati sātvata-puṅgave vibhūmnisva-sukham upagate kvacid vihartuṁprakṛtim upeyuṣi yad-bhava-pravāhaḥ
verses.text: Bhīṣmadeva said: Let me now invest my thinking, feeling and willing, which were so long engaged in different subjects and occupational duties, in the all-powerful Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa. He is always self-satisfied, but sometimes, being the leader of the devotees, He enjoys transcendental pleasure by descending to the material world, although from Him only the material world is created.

verses.sanskrit: त्रिभुवनकमनं तमालवर्णंरविकरगौरवराम्बरं दधाने ।वपुरलककुलावृताननाब्जंविजयसखे रतिरस्तु मेऽनवद्या ॥ ३३ ॥
verses.transliteration: tri-bhuvana-kamanaṁ tamāla-varṇaṁravi-kara-gaura-vara-ambaraṁ dadhānevapur alaka-kulāvṛtānanābjaṁvijaya-sakhe ratir astu me ’navadyā
verses.text: Śrī Kṛṣṇa is the intimate friend of Arjuna. He has appeared on this earth in His transcendental body, which resembles the bluish color of the tamāla tree. His body attracts everyone in the three planetary systems [upper, middle and lower]. May His glittering yellow dress and His lotus face, covered with paintings of sandalwood pulp, be the object of my attraction, and may I not desire fruitive results.

verses.sanskrit: युधि तुरगरजोविधूम्रविष्वक्-कचलुलितश्रमवार्यलङ्‍कृतास्ये ।मम निशितशरैर्विभिद्यमान-त्वचि विलसत्कवचेऽस्तु कृष्ण आत्मा ॥ ३४ ॥
verses.transliteration: yudhi turaga-rajo-vidhūmra-viṣvak-kaca-lulita-śramavāry-alaṅkṛtāsyemama niśita-śarair vibhidyamāna-tvaci vilasat-kavace ’stu kṛṣṇa ātmā
verses.text: On the battlefield [where Śrī Kṛṣṇa attended Arjuna out of friendship], the flowing hair of Lord Kṛṣṇa turned ashen due to the dust raised by the hoofs of the horses. And because of His labor, beads of sweat wetted His face. All these decorations, intensified by the wounds dealt by my sharp arrows, were enjoyed by Him. Let my mind thus go unto Śrī Kṛṣṇa.

verses.sanskrit: सपदि सखिवचो निशम्य मध्येनिजपरयोर्बलयो रथं निवेश्य ।स्थितवति परसैनिकायुरक्ष्णाहृतवति पार्थसखे रतिर्ममास्तु ॥ ३५ ॥
verses.transliteration: sapadi sakhi-vaco niśamya madhyenija-parayor balayo rathaṁ niveśyasthitavati para-sainikāyur akṣṇāhṛtavati pārtha-sakhe ratir mamāstu
verses.text: In obedience to the command of His friend, Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa entered the arena of the Battlefield of Kurukṣetra between the soldiers of Arjuna and Duryodhana, and while there He shortened the life spans of the opposite party by His merciful glance. This was done simply by His looking at the enemy. Let my mind be fixed upon that Kṛṣṇa.

verses.sanskrit: व्यवहितपृतनामुखं निरीक्ष्यस्वजनवधाद्विमुखस्य दोषबुद्ध्या ।कुमतिमहरदात्मविद्यया य-श्चरणरति: परमस्य तस्य मेऽस्तु ॥ ३६ ॥
verses.transliteration: vyavahita-pṛtanā-mukhaṁ nirīkṣyasva-jana-vadhād vimukhasya doṣa-buddhyākumatim aharad ātma-vidyayā yaścaraṇa-ratiḥ paramasya tasya me ’stu
verses.text: When Arjuna was seemingly polluted by ignorance upon observing the soldiers and commanders before him on the battlefield, the Lord eradicated his ignorance by delivering transcendental knowledge. May His lotus feet always remain the object of my attraction.

verses.sanskrit: स्वनिगममपहाय मत्प्रतिज्ञा-मृतमधिकर्तुमवप्लुतो रथस्थ: ।धृतरथचरणोऽभ्ययाच्चलद्गु-र्हरिरिव हन्तुमिभं गतोत्तरीय: ॥ ३७ ॥
verses.transliteration: sva-nigamam apahāya mat-pratijñāmṛtam adhikartum avapluto rathasthaḥdhṛta-ratha-caraṇo ’bhyayāc caladgurharir iva hantum ibhaṁ gatottarīyaḥ
verses.text: Fulfilling my vow and sacrificing His own promise, He got down from the chariot, took up its wheel, and ran towards me hurriedly, just as a lion goes to kill an elephant. He even dropped His outer garment on the way.

verses.sanskrit: शितविशिखहतो विशीर्णदंश:क्षतजपरिप्लुत आततायिनो मे ।प्रसभमभिससार मद्वधार्थंस भवतु मे भगवान् गतिर्मुकुन्द: ॥ ३८ ॥
verses.transliteration: śita-viśikha-hato viśīrṇa-daṁśaḥkṣataja-paripluta ātatāyino meprasabham abhisasāra mad-vadhārthaṁsa bhavatu me bhagavān gatir mukundaḥ
verses.text: May He, Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa, the Personality of Godhead, who awards salvation, be my ultimate destination. On the battlefield He charged me, as if angry because of the wounds dealt by my sharp arrows. His shield was scattered, and His body was smeared with blood due to the wounds.

verses.sanskrit: विजयरथकुटुम्ब आत्ततोत्रेधृतहयरश्मिनि तच्छ्रियेक्षणीये ।भगवति रतिरस्तु मे मुमूर्षो-र्यमिह निरीक्ष्य हता गता: स्वरूपम् ॥ ३९ ॥
verses.transliteration: vijaya-ratha-kuṭumbha ātta-totredhṛta-haya-raśmini tac-chriyekṣaṇīyebhagavati ratir astu me mumūrṣoryam iha nirīkṣya hatā gatāḥ sva-rūpam
verses.text: At the moment of death, let my ultimate attraction be to Śrī Kṛṣṇa, the Personality of Godhead. I concentrate my mind upon the chariot driver of Arjuna who stood with a whip in His right hand and a bridle rope in His left, who was very careful to give protection to Arjuna’s chariot by all means. Those who saw Him on the Battlefield of Kurukṣetra attained their original forms after death.

verses.sanskrit: ललितगतिविलासवल्गुहास-प्रणयनिरीक्षणकल्पितोरुमाना: ।कृतमनुकृतवत्य उन्मदान्धा:प्रकृतिमगन् किल यस्य गोपवध्व: ॥ ४० ॥
verses.transliteration: lalita-gati-vilāsa-valguhāsa-praṇaya-nirīkṣaṇa-kalpitorumānāḥkṛtam anukṛtavatya unmadāndhāḥprakṛtim agan kila yasya gopa-vadhvaḥ
verses.text: Let my mind be fixed upon Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa, whose motions and smiles of love attracted the damsels of Vrajadhāma [the gopīs]. The damsels imitated the characteristic movements of the Lord [after His disappearance from the rāsa dance].

verses.sanskrit: मुनिगणनृपवर्यसङ्कुलेऽन्त:सदसि युधिष्ठिरराजसूय एषाम् ।अर्हणमुपपेद ईक्षणीयोमम द‍ृशिगोचर एष आविरात्मा ॥ ४१ ॥
verses.transliteration: muni-gaṇa-nṛpa-varya-saṅkule ’ntaḥ-sadasi yudhiṣṭhira-rājasūya eṣāmarhaṇam upapeda īkṣaṇīyomama dṛśi-gocara eṣa āvir ātmā
verses.text: At the Rājasūya-yajña [sacrifice] performed by Mahārāja Yudhiṣṭhira, there was the greatest assembly of all the elite men of the world, the royal and learned orders, and in that great assembly Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa was worshiped by one and all as the most exalted Personality of Godhead. This happened during my presence, and I remembered the incident in order to keep my mind upon the Lord.

verses.sanskrit: तमिममहमजं शरीरभाजांहृदि हृदि धिष्ठितमात्मकल्पितानाम् ।प्रतिद‍ृशमिव नैकधार्कमेकंसमधिगतोऽस्मि विधूतभेदमोह: ॥ ४२ ॥
verses.transliteration: tam imam aham ajaṁ śarīra-bhājāṁhṛdi hṛdi dhiṣṭhitam ātma-kalpitānāmpratidṛśam iva naikadhārkam ekaṁsamadhi-gato ’smi vidhūta-bheda-mohaḥ
verses.text: Now I can meditate with full concentration upon that one Lord, Śrī Kṛṣṇa, now present before me because now I have transcended the misconceptions of duality in regard to His presence in everyone’s heart, even in the hearts of the mental speculators. He is in everyone’s heart. The sun may be perceived differently, but the sun is one.

verses.sanskrit: सूत उवाचकृष्ण एवं भगवति मनोवाग्द‍ृष्टिवृत्तिभि: ।आत्मन्यात्मानमावेश्य सोऽन्त:श्वास उपारमत् ॥ ४३ ॥
verses.transliteration: sūta uvācakṛṣṇa evaṁ bhagavatimano-vāg-dṛṣṭi-vṛttibhiḥātmany ātmānam āveśyaso ’ntaḥśvāsa upāramat
verses.text: Sūta Gosvāmī said: Thus Bhīṣmadeva merged himself in the Supersoul, Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, with his mind, speech, sight and actions, and thus he became silent, and his breathing stopped.

verses.sanskrit: सम्पद्यमानमाज्ञाय भीष्मं ब्रह्मणि निष्कले ।सर्वे बभूवुस्ते तूष्णीं वयांसीव दिनात्यये ॥ ४४ ॥
verses.transliteration: sampadyamānam ājñāyabhīṣmaṁ brahmaṇi niṣkalesarve babhūvus te tūṣṇīṁvayāṁsīva dinātyaye
verses.text: Knowing that Bhīṣmadeva had merged into the unlimited eternity of the Supreme Absolute, all present there became silent like birds at the end of the day.

verses.sanskrit: तत्र दुन्दुभयो नेदुर्देवमानववादिता: ।शशंसु: साधवो राज्ञां खात्पेतु: पुष्पवृष्टय: ॥ ४५ ॥
verses.transliteration: tatra dundubhayo nedurdeva-mānava-vāditāḥśaśaṁsuḥ sādhavo rājñāṁkhāt petuḥ puṣpa-vṛṣṭayaḥ
verses.text: Thereafter, both men and demigods sounded drums in honor, and the honest royal order commenced demonstrations of honor and respect. And from the sky fell showers of flowers.

verses.sanskrit: तस्य निर्हरणादीनि सम्परेतस्य भार्गव ।युधिष्ठिर: कारयित्वा मुहूर्तं दु:खितोऽभवत् ॥ ४६ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasya nirharaṇādīnisamparetasya bhārgavayudhiṣṭhiraḥ kārayitvāmuhūrtaṁ duḥkhito ’bhavat
verses.text: O descendant of Bhṛgu [Śaunaka], after performing funeral rituals for the dead body of Bhīṣmadeva, Mahārāja Yudhiṣṭhira was momentarily overtaken with grief.

verses.sanskrit: तुष्टुवुर्मुनयो हृष्टा: कृष्णं तद्गुह्यनामभि: ।ततस्ते कृष्णहृदया: स्वाश्रमान् प्रययु: पुन: ॥ ४७ ॥
verses.transliteration: tuṣṭuvur munayo hṛṣṭāḥkṛṣṇaṁ tad-guhya-nāmabhiḥtatas te kṛṣṇa-hṛdayāḥsvāśramān prayayuḥ punaḥ
verses.text: All the great sages then glorified Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa, who was present there, by confidential Vedic hymns. Then all of them returned to their respective hermitages, bearing always Lord Kṛṣṇa within their hearts.

verses.sanskrit: ततो युधिष्ठिरो गत्वा सहकृष्णो गजाह्वयम् ।पितरं सान्‍त्वयामास गान्धारीं च तपस्विनीम् ॥ ४८ ॥
verses.transliteration: tato yudhiṣṭhiro gatvāsaha-kṛṣṇo gajāhvayampitaraṁ sāntvayām āsagāndhārīṁ ca tapasvinīm
verses.text: Thereafter, Mahārāja Yudhiṣṭhira at once went to his capital, Hastināpura, accompanied by Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa, and there he consoled his uncle and aunt Gāndhārī, who was an ascetic.

verses.sanskrit: पित्रा चानुमतो राजा वासुदेवानुमोदित: ।चकार राज्यं धर्मेण पितृपैतामहं विभु: ॥ ४९ ॥
verses.transliteration: pitrā cānumato rājāvāsudevānumoditaḥcakāra rājyaṁ dharmeṇapitṛ-paitāmahaṁ vibhuḥ
verses.text: After this, the great religious King, Mahārāja Yudhiṣṭhira, executed the royal power in the kingdom strictly according to the codes of royal principles approved by his uncle and confirmed by Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa.
